_doc: Neft Teacher — per-lesson graphic-novel rollout manifest. Extends the per-UNIT novels to a finer per-lesson grain. Generated/consumed by scripts/generate-lesson-novels.mjs.
_groupingLogic: Lessons are read from lessons/<id>/config.json (non-flagship) and sorted by unit then lesson. Consecutive lessons in the SAME unit that share the SAME full standard code (e.g. both 6.NS.3) are merged into one 2-lesson novel group; otherwise a lesson stands alone as a 1-lesson group. This yields 46 groups across the 64 lessons. groupId = u<unit>-l<lesson>[l<lesson>]. Each group reuses its unit's existing theme/themeEmoji from the configs (e.g. Unit 1 = space-station). Per group the generator emits Graphic Novel #1 (Support) and #2 (Enrichment).
_artNote: Art is referenced by path under graphic-novels/_art/lessons/<groupId>/ (cover.png, act1.png, act2.png, final.png, complete.png). Until those PNGs exist the novels use the same graceful diagonal-stripe dark fallback as units 8/9, showing the alt text. Mass art generation is DEFERRED because the image API is spend-capped.
_rollout: 2 of 46 groups are flagged generate:true as working samples (u1-l5l6 and u1-l7 — the Unit 1 decimal lessons not covered by the existing anchor novels). The remaining 44 are data-only stubs (generate:false, novels:[]); author their novels[] with the same shape and flip generate:true to mass-run once the art budget reopens.

groups.title: Add and Subtract Decimals / Multiply Decimals

cover.artAlt: A space cadet stands at a glowing docking-bay console covered in decimal fuel readouts
cover.blurb: The station's fuel computer is glitching! Only correct decimal math can dock the supply ship. Line up those decimal points and save the crew, Cadet.
cover.blurbEs: ¡La computadora de combustible falla! Solo las matematicas con decimales pueden acoplar la nave. Alinea los puntos decimales y salva a la tripulacion.

acts.title: Fuel Totals
acts.artAlt: Two fuel tanks with decimal gauges glowing on the bridge

dialogue.en: Cadet! Tank A holds 3.4 liters, Tank B holds 2.75 liters. I need the TOTAL fuel to dock.
dialogue.es: ¡Cadete! El tanque A tiene 3.4 litros y el B tiene 2.75. Necesito el TOTAL para acoplar.

dialogue.en: To add decimals I line up the decimal points first. I can annex a zero so 3.4 becomes 3.40.
dialogue.es: Para sumar decimales alineo los puntos primero. Puedo agregar un cero: 3.4 es 3.40.

interlude.en: Total locked in! Now a leak — I must subtract what we lost.
interlude.es: ¡Total listo! Ahora hay una fuga: debo restar lo que perdimos.

challenges.prompt: Add the two tanks: 3.4 + 2.75. Line up the decimal points. What is the total?
challenges.promptEs: Suma los dos tanques: 3.4 + 2.75. Alinea los puntos. ¿Cuál es el total?
challenges.hint: Annex a zero so both have 2 places: 3.40 + 2.75. Add hundredths, tenths, then ones.
challenges.frame: I lined up the decimal points and added to get ____ liters.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Correct! 3.40 + 2.75 = 6.15 liters. Lining up the points keeps each place value matched.
challenges.badEn: ❌ Check your places. Annex a zero (3.40) and line up the decimal points, then add.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Revisa los valores posicionales. Agrega un cero (3.40) y alinea los puntos.

challenges.prompt: A leak drains fuel. Start with 8.5 liters and subtract 3.27 liters. How much is left?
challenges.promptEs: Una fuga drena combustible. De 8.5 litros resta 3.27. ¿Cuánto queda?
challenges.hint: Annex a zero: 8.50 - 3.27. Line up the points and subtract place by place.
challenges.frame: After lining up the points, 8.50 - 3.27 = ____ liters.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Yes! 8.50 - 3.27 = 5.23 liters. The decimal points stay lined up in the answer too.
challenges.badEn: ❌ Annex a zero (8.50) and subtract place by place, keeping the decimal points aligned.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Agrega un cero (8.50) y resta posición por posición.

acts.title: Power Boost
acts.artAlt: A glowing reactor core multiplying decimal energy values

dialogue.en: Docking complete. Now multiply decimals to set the reactor power. No lining up needed — count the decimal places!
dialogue.es: Acople listo. Ahora multiplica decimales para la potencia. No alineas: ¡cuenta los lugares decimales!

dialogue.en: Right — I multiply like whole numbers, then place the decimal point using the total number of decimal places.
dialogue.es: Claro: multiplico como enteros y luego coloco el punto usando el total de lugares decimales.

interlude.en: Core stable! One more: a smaller setting with extra decimal places.
interlude.es: ¡Núcleo estable! Una más: un ajuste pequeño con más decimales.

challenges.prompt: Set the reactor: 0.6 × 0.4. Multiply, then place the decimal point. What is the product?
challenges.promptEs: Ajusta el reactor: 0.6 × 0.4. Multiplica y coloca el punto. ¿Cuál es el producto?
challenges.hint: 6 × 4 = 24. There is 1 decimal place in 0.6 and 1 in 0.4 — that's 2 places total, so the product has 2 places.
challenges.frame: 0.6 has 1 place and 0.4 has 1 place, so the product 0.24 has ____ decimal places.
challenges.badEn: ❌ Multiply 6×4=24, then count decimal places: 1+1=2, so the answer is 0.24.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Multiplica 6×4=24 y cuenta los lugares: 1+1=2, así que es 0.24.

challenges.prompt: Fine-tune the power: 1.2 × 0.05. Multiply, then place the decimal point correctly.
challenges.promptEs: Ajuste fino: 1.2 × 0.05. Multiplica y coloca bien el punto.
challenges.hint: 12 × 5 = 60. Places: 1 (in 1.2) + 2 (in 0.05) = 3. You may need to annex a zero in front.
challenges.frame: 1.2 (1 place) × 0.05 (2 places) needs ____ decimal places, so the product is 0.06.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Yes! 12×5=60; with 3 decimal places that is 0.060 = 0.06. Reactor tuned!

acts.title: The Launch Code
acts.artAlt: A glowing launch console asking for one combined decimal code

dialogue.en: Final launch code, Cadet! It uses BOTH skills — add, then multiply.
dialogue.es: ¡Código final, Cadete! Usa AMBAS destrezas: suma y luego multiplica.

dialogue.en: First I add 4.5 + 1.5, then multiply the sum by 0.5. Order matters!
dialogue.es: Primero sumo 4.5 + 1.5, luego multiplico por 0.5. ¡El orden importa!

challenges.prompt: Compute (4.5 + 1.5) × 0.5. Add first, then multiply. Which line is fully correct?
challenges.promptEs: Calcula (4.5 + 1.5) × 0.5. Suma primero, luego multiplica. ¿Cuál línea es correcta?
challenges.goodEn: ✅ CODE ACCEPTED! 6.0 × 0.5 = 3.0 — exactly half of 6. Launch sequence go!
challenges.badEn: ❌ Add first: 4.5 + 1.5 = 6.0. Then 6.0 × 0.5 is HALF of 6, which is 3.0 — not 30.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Suma primero: 4.5 + 1.5 = 6.0. Luego × 0.5 es la MITAD de 6, o sea 3.0.

complete.artAlt: The cadet watches the supply ship launch safely into a star field
complete.heading: Mission Complete!
complete.text: You added and subtracted decimals to dock the ship and multiplied decimals to power the launch. The station is fueled and flying. Great work, Cadet!
complete.textEs: ¡Sumaste, restaste y multiplicaste decimales para salvar la estación! Excelente trabajo.

bonus.prompt: Master check: 2.5 × 0.4. Multiply, then place the decimal point. What is the product?
bonus.promptEs: Reto maestro: 2.5 × 0.4. Multiplica y coloca el punto. ¿Cuál es el producto?

cover.artAlt: A confident cadet commands a deep-space station with multi-step decimal mission boards
cover.blurb: Command rank, Cadet. These missions chain several decimal operations and ask you to reason about place value, not just compute. Think it through and launch the fleet.
cover.blurbEs: Rango de mando, Cadete. Estas misiones encadenan operaciones con decimales y piden razonar sobre el valor posicional. Piensa bien y lanza la flota.

acts.title: The Supply Manifest
acts.artAlt: A holographic manifest listing several decimal supply masses

dialogue.en: Commander, three crates: 2.45 kg, 1.8 kg, and 0.75 kg. I need the exact combined mass.
dialogue.es: Comandante, tres cajas: 2.45 kg, 1.8 kg y 0.75 kg. Necesito la masa combinada exacta.

dialogue.en: I'll annex zeros so every value has two decimal places, then add carefully.
dialogue.es: Agregaré ceros para que todos tengan dos decimales y luego sumaré con cuidado.

interlude.en: Manifest confirmed. Now find the difference from our 6 kg limit.
interlude.es: Manifiesto confirmado. Ahora halla la diferencia con el límite de 6 kg.

challenges.prompt: Add all three crates: 2.45 + 1.8 + 0.75. What is the total mass?
challenges.promptEs: Suma las tres cajas: 2.45 + 1.8 + 0.75. ¿Cuál es la masa total?
challenges.hint: Annex a zero so 1.8 = 1.80. Then 2.45 + 1.80 + 0.75. Add the hundredths first.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Exactly! 2.45 + 1.80 + 0.75 = 5.00 kg. Annexing 1.8 → 1.80 kept the places aligned.
challenges.badEn: ❌ Annex a zero (1.80) and add the hundredths column first: 5 + 0 + 5 = 10, carry the tenth.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Agrega un cero (1.80) y suma primero las centésimas.

challenges.prompt: The cargo limit is 6 kg. With 5.00 kg loaded, how much more mass can you add?
challenges.promptEs: El límite es 6 kg. Con 5.00 kg cargados, ¿cuánta masa más cabe?
challenges.hint: Subtract: 6.00 - 5.00. Annex zeros so both have two decimal places.

acts.title: Thruster Calibration
acts.artAlt: A cadet calibrating thrusters with decimal multiplier dials

dialogue.en: Each thruster uses 0.25 units of plasma. We are firing 1.2 thruster-seconds. How much plasma?
dialogue.es: Cada propulsor usa 0.25 de plasma. Encenderemos 1.2 segundos. ¿Cuánto plasma?

dialogue.en: Multiply like whole numbers, then count three decimal places total.
dialogue.es: Multiplico como enteros y cuento tres lugares decimales en total.

interlude.en: Calibrated! Now scale it up for the whole array.
interlude.es: ¡Calibrado! Ahora escálalo para todo el arreglo.

challenges.prompt: Compute 1.2 × 0.25 to find the plasma used. What is the product?
challenges.promptEs: Calcula 1.2 × 0.25 para el plasma usado. ¿Cuál es el producto?
challenges.frame: 12 × 25 = 300, and with 3 decimal places that is 0.3 units.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Yes! 12×25=300; 3 decimal places gives 0.300 = 0.3 units of plasma.

challenges.prompt: The array has 4 thrusters. If one uses 0.3 units, the array uses 4 × 0.3. How much plasma total?
challenges.promptEs: El arreglo tiene 4 propulsores. Si uno usa 0.3, el arreglo usa 4 × 0.3. ¿Cuánto total?
challenges.hint: 4 × 3 = 12, and 0.3 has 1 decimal place, so the product has 1 decimal place: 1.2.
challenges.frame: 4 × 0.3 = ____ units of plasma for the whole array.

acts.title: The Fleet Launch Code
acts.artAlt: A commander entering a multi-step decimal launch code for a fleet

dialogue.en: Final code, Commander: it chains a sum and a product. Reason carefully.
dialogue.es: Código final, Comandante: encadena una suma y un producto. Razona con cuidado.

dialogue.en: Add 3.25 + 0.75 first, then multiply that sum by 0.5.
dialogue.es: Sumo 3.25 + 0.75 primero, luego multiplico por 0.5.

challenges.prompt: Compute (3.25 + 0.75) × 0.5. Which line is fully correct?
challenges.goodEn: ✅ FLEET CODE ACCEPTED! 4.00 × 0.5 = 2.0 — exactly half of 4. The fleet launches!
challenges.badEn: ❌ Add first: 3.25 + 0.75 = 4.00. Then × 0.5 is HALF of 4, which is 2.0 — not 20.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Suma primero: 3.25 + 0.75 = 4.00. Luego × 0.5 es la MITAD de 4, o sea 2.0.

complete.artAlt: A proud commander salutes as a fleet of ships launches into deep space
complete.heading: Fleet Launched!
complete.text: You chained decimal addition, subtraction, and multiplication across the whole mission. Command rank earned, Commander!
complete.textEs: ¡Encadenaste sumas, restas y multiplicaciones con decimales en toda la misión! Rango de mando ganado.

bonus.prompt: Command challenge: (1.5 + 2.5) × 0.25. What is the result?
bonus.promptEs: Reto de mando: (1.5 + 2.5) × 0.25. ¿Cuál es el resultado?

cover.artAlt: A space cadet at a navigation drive that divides decimal distances into equal jumps
cover.blurb: The hyperdrive splits long decimal distances into equal jumps. To divide with decimals, make the divisor a whole number first. Pilot us home, Cadet!
cover.blurbEs: El hiperimpulsor divide distancias decimales en saltos iguales. Para dividir, primero haz que el divisor sea entero. ¡Llévanos a casa, Cadete!

acts.title: Equal Jumps
acts.artAlt: A navigation screen splitting a decimal distance into equal jump markers

dialogue.en: Cadet, we must cross 4.5 light-units in jumps of 0.5 each. How MANY jumps?
dialogue.es: Cadete, cruzamos 4.5 unidades en saltos de 0.5. ¿CUÁNTOS saltos?

dialogue.en: To divide by a decimal, I make the divisor whole. Multiply BOTH numbers by 10: 4.5 ÷ 0.5 becomes 45 ÷ 5.
dialogue.es: Para dividir entre un decimal, hago el divisor entero. Multiplico AMBOS por 10: 4.5 ÷ 0.5 es 45 ÷ 5.

interlude.en: Course plotted! Now a longer leg with a tighter jump.
interlude.es: ¡Rumbo trazado! Ahora un tramo más largo con saltos más cortos.

challenges.lockTitle: 🚀 Jump Lock #1 — Divide by a Decimal
challenges.prompt: Compute 4.5 ÷ 0.5. Make the divisor a whole number first. How many jumps?
challenges.promptEs: Calcula 4.5 ÷ 0.5. Haz el divisor entero primero. ¿Cuántos saltos?
challenges.frame: I multiplied both numbers by 10, so 4.5 ÷ 0.5 = 45 ÷ 5 = ____.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Correct! Multiply both by 10: 45 ÷ 5 = 9 jumps. Same answer, easier division.
challenges.badEn: ❌ Make the divisor whole: multiply both by 10 to get 45 ÷ 5 = 9.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Haz el divisor entero: ×10 en ambos para 45 ÷ 5 = 9.

challenges.prompt: Now cross 9.6 light-units in jumps of 0.8. Compute 9.6 ÷ 0.8.
challenges.promptEs: Ahora cruza 9.6 unidades en saltos de 0.8. Calcula 9.6 ÷ 0.8.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Yes! 96 ÷ 8 = 12 jumps. The decimal point moved the same way in both numbers.

acts.title: The Home Jump
acts.artAlt: The final hyperspace jump home shown as a decimal division on the console

dialogue.en: Final leg, Cadet: 7.2 light-units in jumps of 0.9. Get us home!
dialogue.es: Tramo final, Cadete: 7.2 unidades en saltos de 0.9. ¡Llévanos a casa!

dialogue.en: Multiply both by 10 and divide. Here we go!
dialogue.es: Multiplico ambos por 10 y divido. ¡Vamos!

challenges.prompt: Compute 7.2 ÷ 0.9. Make the divisor whole first. Which line is correct?
challenges.promptEs: Calcula 7.2 ÷ 0.9. Haz el divisor entero. ¿Cuál línea es correcta?
challenges.goodEn: ✅ HOME JUMP LOCKED! 72 ÷ 9 = 8 jumps. You moved the decimal in BOTH numbers. We're home!
challenges.badEn: ❌ Move the decimal in BOTH numbers (×10): 7.2 ÷ 0.9 = 72 ÷ 9 = 8, not 0.8.
challenges.badEs: ❌ Mueve el punto en AMBOS números (×10): 72 ÷ 9 = 8, no 0.8.

complete.artAlt: The cadet arrives home as the station glows safely in orbit
complete.heading: Mission Complete!
complete.text: You divided decimals by making each divisor a whole number, then dividing. Every jump landed perfectly and you piloted the crew home. Great work, Cadet!
complete.textEs: ¡Dividiste decimales haciendo el divisor entero y luego dividiendo! Llevaste a la tripulación a casa.

bonus.prompt: Master check: 6.4 ÷ 0.4. Make the divisor whole first. What is the quotient?
bonus.promptEs: Reto maestro: 6.4 ÷ 0.4. Haz el divisor entero. ¿Cuál es el cociente?

cover.artAlt: A commander planning multi-jump routes that divide decimal distances and check the math
cover.blurb: Command rank, Cadet. Plan multi-jump routes and reason about why moving the decimal keeps the quotient the same. Then check your own answer with multiplication.
cover.blurbEs: Rango de mando, Cadete. Planea rutas de varios saltos y razona por qué mover el punto no cambia el cociente. Luego comprueba con multiplicación.

acts.title: Route Planning
acts.artAlt: A holographic route map dividing a long decimal distance into equal legs

dialogue.en: Commander, plan a route of 12.6 light-units in legs of 0.6 each. How many legs?
dialogue.es: Comandante, planea una ruta de 12.6 unidades en tramos de 0.6. ¿Cuántos tramos?

dialogue.en: Multiply both by 10 to get 126 ÷ 6. Moving the decimal the same way in both keeps the quotient identical.
dialogue.es: Multiplico ambos por 10: 126 ÷ 6. Mover el punto igual en ambos no cambia el cociente.

interlude.en: Route set. Now I'll check it with multiplication.
interlude.es: Ruta lista. Ahora la compruebo con multiplicación.

challenges.prompt: Compute 12.6 ÷ 0.6 to count the legs. Make the divisor whole first.
challenges.promptEs: Calcula 12.6 ÷ 0.6 para contar los tramos. Haz el divisor entero primero.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Exactly! 126 ÷ 6 = 21 legs. Same decimal shift in both keeps the quotient equal.

challenges.prompt: Check your answer: if 12.6 ÷ 0.6 = 21, then 21 × 0.6 should equal 12.6. What is 21 × 0.6?
challenges.promptEs: Comprueba: si 12.6 ÷ 0.6 = 21, entonces 21 × 0.6 debe ser 12.6. ¿Cuánto es 21 × 0.6?
challenges.frame: 21 × 0.6 = ____, which matches the original distance, so the division checks out.
challenges.goodEn: ✅ Yes! 21 × 0.6 = 12.6 matches the original distance — your division is confirmed.
challenges.badEn: ❌ 21 × 6 = 126; with 1 decimal place that is 12.6, which matches the start.

acts.title: The Precision Jump
acts.artAlt: A precise final jump computed by dividing a hundredths decimal distance

dialogue.en: Precision leg, Commander: 1.25 light-units in jumps of 0.25. How many?
dialogue.es: Tramo de precisión, Comandante: 1.25 unidades en saltos de 0.25. ¿Cuántos?

dialogue.en: These have hundredths, so I multiply both by 100 to make the divisor whole.
dialogue.es: Tienen centésimas, así que multiplico ambos por 100 para hacer el divisor entero.

challenges.prompt: Compute 1.25 ÷ 0.25. The divisor has hundredths. Which line is correct?
challenges.promptEs: Calcula 1.25 ÷ 0.25. El divisor tiene centésimas. ¿Cuál línea es correcta?
challenges.goodEn: ✅ PRECISION JUMP LOCKED! Multiply both by 100: 125 ÷ 25 = 5 jumps. Flawless navigation, Commander!
challenges.badEn: ❌ Both numbers have hundredths — multiply both by 100: 125 ÷ 25 = 5, not 50.

complete.artAlt: A commander confirms the fleet's safe arrival after precise decimal jumps
complete.heading: Precision Achieved!
complete.text: You divided decimals by shifting the point in both numbers, checked your work by multiplying, and even handled hundredths. Command rank earned, Commander!
complete.textEs: ¡Dividiste decimales moviendo el punto en ambos, comprobaste multiplicando y manejaste centésimas! Rango de mando ganado.

bonus.prompt: Command challenge: 3.6 ÷ 0.12. Make the divisor whole (×100). What is the quotient?
bonus.promptEs: Reto de mando: 3.6 ÷ 0.12. Haz el divisor entero (×100). ¿Cuál es el cociente?

groups.title: Interpret Division of Fractions / Divide Whole Numbers by Fractions
Use Ratio Reasoning / Ratio and Rate Problem Solving
Relate Fractions, Decimals, and Percents / Percents Greater Than 100% and Less Than 1%
Find the Percent of a Number / Use Percent to Solve Problems
Area of Parallelograms / Area of Trapezoids
Area of Triangles / Area of Regular Polygons
Properties of Operations / The Distributive Property
Solve One-Step Addition and Subtraction Equations / Solve Multiplication and Division Equations
Display Data: Box Plots / Display Data: Histograms
Integers and Absolute Value / Compare and Order Integers
Rational Numbers on the Number Line
Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants
Volume with Whole Number Edges / Volume of Rectangular Prisms
Surface Area Using Nets / Surface Area of Prisms
